statistics.normality_note: Note: Shapiro-Wilk test. P-value < 0.05 indicates non-normal distribution.
statistics.deficit_note: Negative values indicate water deficit (ETo > Precipitation). Positive values indicate surplus.
statistics.forecast_sample_insufficient: Test not performed: insufficient sample size for forecast mode (6 days). Statistics such as CV (%), Skewness, Kurtosis and Shapiro-Wilk require larger samples for reliable results.
statistics.sample_insufficient: Not calculated: statistics such as CV (%), Skewness, Kurtosis and Shapiro-Wilk require at least 30 days of data for reliable results. Currently: {days} days.
statistics.heatmap_insufficient: Correlation heatmap not shown: at least 30 days of data are required for reliable correlations. Currently: {days} days.
statistics.forecast_heatmap_insufficient: Correlation heatmap not shown: insufficient sample for reliable correlations in forecast mode (6 days).

about.page_subtitle: Open-source tool for reference evapotranspiration estimation
about.hero_description: EVAonline is an open-source, no-installation web platform for daily reference evapotranspiration (ET₀) estimation worldwide. It integrates six public reanalysis and forecast APIs (NASA POWER, Open-Meteo, NWS, MET Norway, and others) through a hexagonal architecture and a two-stage fusion strategy: physically informed weighted averaging followed by an adaptive Kalman filter constrained by 30-year regional climatology. Validated over 30 years (1991–2020) against the BR-DWGD benchmark at 17 Brazilian cities (16 in the MATOPIBA region + Piracicaba/SP), it achieved KGE = 0.814 and MAE = 0.423 mm day⁻¹ — reducing error by ≈50% and bias by 95.5% compared to any individual source.
about.method_fao56_desc: The internationally recognized standard method for estimating reference evapotranspiration (ET₀), recommended by the Food and Agriculture Organization of the United Nations (FAO). Uses temperature, humidity, wind speed, and solar radiation as inputs.
about.method_kalman_desc: An ensemble approach that combines multiple climate data sources using adaptive Kalman filtering. The system learns from 1991–2020 climatological normals to dynamically adjust weights, minimizing bias and improving accuracy compared to any individual source.
about.method_kalman_highlight: 38–51% MAE reduction and 91–95% bias reduction vs individual sources
about.validation_desc: Validated against Xavier et al. BR-DWGD reference dataset (3,625+ weather stations, 0.1° resolution). Study: 17 Brazilian cities (16 in MATOPIBA + Piracicaba/SP), 30 years (1991–2020), 186,287 daily observations.
about.val_reference: Xavier, A. C., Scanlon, B. R., King, C. W., & Alves, A. I. (2022). New improved Brazilian daily weather gridded data (1961–2020). International Journal of Climatology, 42(16), 8390–8404. https://doi.org/10.1002/joc.7731
about.data_sources_desc: EVAonline integrates 7 data sources (6 climate + 1 elevation), automatically selecting the best combination based on geographic location and data availability.
about.ds_ref_openmeteo: Zippenfenig, P. (2023). Open-Meteo.com Weather API [Computer software]. Zenodo. https://doi.org/10.5281/ZENODO.7970649
about.ds_ref_nasapower: The data was obtained from NASA Langley Research Center’s Prediction Of Worldwide Energy Resources (POWER) project, funded through the NASA Earth Science Division.
about.affiliation_usp: Dept. of Biosystems Engineering, University of São Paulo, Piracicaba, Brazil
about.affiliation_unesp: School of Engineering, São Paulo State University, Guaratinguétá, Brazil
about.affiliation_embrapa: Geoprocessing Laboratory, Brazilian Agricultural Research Corporation, Corumbá, Brazil
about.license_desc: GNU Affero General Public License v3.0 — Free to use, modify and distribute. Modifications must be shared under the same license.
about.repo_desc: Full source code, documentation, and issue tracker. Contributions are welcome via pull requests.

flow.title: End-to-End Data Flow
flow.step3_desc: Source selection → Download → Preprocess → Fusion → ETo
flow.architecture_desc: Clean Hexagonal DDD (Clean + Hexagonal + Domain-Driven Design). Layers: Presentation (Dash) → Application (FastAPI) → Domain (ETo, Kalman) → Infrastructure (APIs, DB, Redis, Celery).

pipeline.title: Data Processing Pipeline
pipeline.description_suffix: . The pipeline is executed by Celery workers asynchronously.
pipeline.step1_desc: Coordinate validation → Date validation → Mode detection → Source selection → Parallel fetch
pipeline.step2_desc: Physical limits (WMO/Xavier) → IQR outlier detection (max 5%) → Linear interpolation → Fill → Mean
pipeline.step3_desc: Historical: per-variable NASA/OM weights (validated). Recent: primary+fallback sources with HIST_WEIGHTS. Forecast: region weights (USA → NWS 50%/OM 30%/MET 20%; Nordic → MET 80%/OM 20%; Global → OM 70%/MET 30%)
pipeline.step4_desc: Stage 1: Adaptive Kalman on precipitation (outlier detection via monthly P01/P99 percentiles, R×500 penalty). Stage 2: Final adaptive Kalman on calculated ETo (monthly bias correction + continuous filter using 1991–2020 climatology)
pipeline.step5_desc: Fusion (step 3) → Load 30-year monthly normals (1991–2020) → Kalman precipitation filter (step 4a) → FAO-56 ETo calculation → Kalman ETo correction (step 4b) → Set fusion_mode

endpoints.title: API Endpoints
endpoints.description_prefix: All API routes are prefixed with
endpoints.desc_eto_calc: Async ETo calculation → task_id + WebSocket URL

database.title: Database Models (PostgreSQL)
database.description: 5 tables managed via SQLAlchemy + Alembic migrations. Connection pooling: 20 pool, 30 overflow, 3600s recycle.
database.strategy_climate: JSONB storage for flexible multi-source data in single table
database.strategy_api_vars: Maps each API's variable names to standardized internal names

celery.title: Celery Workers & Async Tasks
celery.description_suffix: (publishes to Redis pub/sub for WebSocket streaming).
celery.step5: Generate output file (CSV/Excel); email if applicable
celery.step6: Return result dict to Redis backend

websocket.title: WebSocket (Real-time Communication)
websocket.server_subscribe: Server subscribes to Redis pub/sub channel
websocket.server_coroutines: Two coroutines: Redis listener + Celery poller

validation.title: Validation & Scientific Methodology
validation.overview_text: EVAonline was validated against the Brazilian Daily Weather Gridded Data (BR-DWGD) by Xavier et al. (2016, 2022), the gold-standard reference ETo dataset for Brazil. The validation covers 17 sites (16 in the MATOPIBA agricultural frontier + Piracicaba/SP), 30 years of daily data (1991–2020, 10,958 days per site), and compares 4 ETo estimation methods against the BR-DWGD reference.
validation.highlight_text: EVAonline Fusion achieved KGE = 0.814, reducing RMSE by 49% vs NASA POWER and by 34% vs the best single source (Open-Meteo API).
validation.val_step1_desc: Download raw data from NASA POWER (7 variables at 2m) and Open-Meteo Archive (7 variables, wind at 10m). Period: 1991–2020, 17 MATOPIBA cities + Piracicaba/SP.
validation.val_step2_desc: Apply physical limits (Xavier et al., 2016). IQR outlier detection (max 5%). Linear interpolation for gaps. Wind conversion: Open-Meteo 10m → 2m via FAO-56 Eq. 47.
validation.val_step3_desc: Per-variable weighted average: each variable has a NASA weight and an Open-Meteo weight (see table below). Precipitation: simple average (0.50/0.50) + Adaptive Kalman filter for outlier detection.
validation.val_step4_desc: Complete FAO-56 PM calculation on the fused data: saturation/actual vapor pressure, extraterrestrial radiation (Eqs. 21–25), net radiation, psychrometric constant with altitude correction.
validation.val_step5_desc: 3-step adaptive Kalman on calculated ETo: (a) monthly bias correction using 30-year climatology, (b) continuous Kalman filter (no monthly reset) with dynamic P01/P99 bounds, (c) anomaly detection.
validation.val_step6_desc: Compare EVAonline ETo against Xavier et al. (2022) gridded reference dataset (gold standard for Brazil). Calculate R², KGE, NSE, MAE, RMSE, PBIAS across all 17 sites.
validation.weights_desc: In historical mode, each meteorological variable is fused as a weighted average of NASA POWER and Open-Meteo Archive. These weights were optimized to minimize error against Xavier BR-DWGD and are identical in both the validation scripts and the production backend.
validation.weights_note: Precipitation uses simple average (0.50/0.50) followed by Adaptive Kalman filter for outlier detection using monthly percentile bounds.
validation.results_desc: Mean metrics across 17 sites comparing 4 ETo estimation methods against Xavier BR-DWGD reference:
validation.improvement_label: Improvement vs best individual source (Open-Meteo API):
validation.mode_recent_fusion: Fusion: HIST_WEIGHTS when both primary sources available; OM Forecast fills gaps
validation.mode_forecast_fusion: Fusion: Region weights — USA: NWS 50%/OM 30%/MET 20%; Nordic: MET 80%/OM 20%; Global: OM 70%/MET 30%

documentation.step1_desc: Click anywhere on the interactive map, or type coordinates manually. The system automatically detects:
documentation.step2_historical_detail: (1990→present, 2-day delay, up to 90 days, email required),
documentation.step2_forecast_detail: (today → today+5, irrigation planning). The system auto-detects the best mode based on your date selection.
documentation.step3_desc: The system automatically: fetches data from multiple APIs, applies preprocessing (outlier detection, gap filling), fuses sources via
documentation.step3_displays: , and displays interactive results. Real-time progress is shown via WebSocket.
documentation.fully_automatic_desc: Source selection, data fusion, preprocessing, and ETo calculation are entirely automatic. No manual configuration needed.
documentation.map_intro: The interactive world map allows you to select any location by clicking directly on the map. It includes contextual layers for better geographic reference, especially for Brazilian regions.
documentation.map_layer_control_desc: Click the map icon in the top-right corner to toggle contextual layers on/off.
documentation.map_geolocation_desc: Click the arrow icon in the top-left corner to automatically detect your current location.
documentation.map_layer_estados_desc: Displays the boundaries of all 27 Brazilian states (UFs). Useful for geographic reference when working with locations in Brazil.
documentation.map_layer_matopiba_desc_start: Shows the MATOPIBA agricultural frontier region, covering parts of
documentation.map_layer_matopiba_desc_end: hia states. Important region for Brazilian agribusiness.
documentation.map_layer_cities_desc: Displays 337 municipalities within the MATOPIBA region. Each marker shows city name, coordinates, and elevation on hover.
documentation.map_layer_piracicaba_desc_start: Special marker for Piracicaba/SP, home of
documentation.modes_intro: EVAonline operates in three modes, automatically selected based on the date range you choose. Each mode uses different data sources optimized for that time period.
documentation.mode_historical_desc: Long-term historical analysis for past periods.
documentation.mode_historical_delivery: Processed asynchronously via Celery; results sent by email (CSV/Excel)
documentation.mode_recent_desc: Recent climate data up to today.
documentation.mode_recent_sources: NASA POWER + Open-Meteo Archive + Open-Meteo Forecast (gap filling)
documentation.mode_recent_gap: Open-Meteo Forecast fills the 2-day Archive delay
documentation.mode_forecast_desc: ETo forecast for the next days.
documentation.modes_auto_desc: The system automatically selects the mode based on your dates. Historical mode has a 2-day delay (data not yet available). Recent mode covers up to today. Forecast mode is for future dates.
documentation.usa_intro_start: When you select a point in the
documentation.usa_intro_end: (lat 24°–49°N, lon 66°–125°W), EVAonline automatically activates two additional data sources:
documentation.usa_item1_desc: Official NOAA weather forecast for the grid point
documentation.usa_item2_desc: Finds the nearest active station from ~1,800 NOAA stations
documentation.usa_item3: Fetches hourly real-time observations from that station
documentation.usa_item4: Aggregates hourly data to daily values (Tmax, Tmin, RH, wind, etc.)
documentation.usa_item5: Includes station data in the Kalman fusion as an additional source
documentation.usa_available_desc: Forecast mode only. When activated, an info card displays station name, ID, distance, elevation, and the latest observation values.
documentation.usa_coverage_desc: Continental USA (excluding Alaska, Hawaii). Bounding box: 24°N–49°N, 66°W–125°W.
documentation.results_tabs_desc: Results are organized in interactive tabs. Each table and chart has individual download options.
documentation.tab_tables_3: Descriptive statistics (mean, median, SD, CV%, skewness, kurtosis)
documentation.tab_tables_5: Each table has individual CSV & Excel download buttons
documentation.tab_charts_6: Each chart can be downloaded as PNG/JPG
documentation.charts_desc: All charts are fully interactive: zoom, pan, hover for values. Each chart has PNG and JPG download buttons. Built with Plotly.
documentation.chart_eto_temp_desc: Time series: Tmax, Tmin, and ETo overlay
documentation.chart_eto_rad_desc: Correlation between ETo and solar radiation
documentation.downloads_intro: EVAonline provides comprehensive download options at multiple levels:
documentation.downloads_per_table_desc: Each result table (Climate Data, Statistics, ETo Summary, Normality) has its own CSV and Excel download buttons. This allows you to export exactly the data you need.
documentation.downloads_per_chart_desc: Every chart includes PNG and JPG download buttons for high-resolution images (3840×2160). You can also zoom, pan, and hover for detailed values.
documentation.downloads_global_desc: Download all data at once as CSV or Excel. CSV uses locale-aware formatting (PT: semicolon separator, comma decimal; EN: comma separator, period decimal).
documentation.downloads_email_desc: Historical requests (>30 days) are processed asynchronously. You receive an email with the data file attached when processing is complete (typically 1-5 minutes).
documentation.stats_rules_intro: Some statistical analyses require a minimum sample size for reliable results:
documentation.stats_rule_30days_desc: Statistics such as CV (%), Skewness, Kurtosis, and Shapiro-Wilk normality test require at least 30 days of data. With fewer days, these metrics are not calculated and a notice is shown instead.
documentation.stats_rule_forecast_desc: In Forecast mode (6 days), advanced statistics are not available due to insufficient sample size. Basic metrics (mean, min, max, median, std dev) are always shown.
documentation.stats_rule_heatmap_desc: The correlation heatmap requires at least 30 days of data. For shorter periods, correlations are unreliable and the heatmap is hidden with an informative notice.
documentation.water_deficit_intro: EVAonline calculates a daily water balance based on precipitation minus ETo:
documentation.water_deficit_negative: Negative values indicate water deficit (irrigation needed)
documentation.eto_comparison_desc: Results include both EVAonline ETo (calculated via FAO-56 PM with Kalman fusion) and Open-Meteo reference ETo, allowing direct comparison of methodologies.
documentation.usa_results_desc: When calculating ETo for Continental USA locations, an additional card shows the nearest NWS weather station with:
documentation.ocean_detection_desc: If you click on the ocean or a water body, EVAonline blocks the calculation with a clear warning. ETo is only meaningful for land areas. If the elevation service cannot determine altitude (common for coastal points), elevation defaults to 0m (sea level) with a warning banner.
documentation.variables_intro: These are the climate variables used in the FAO-56 Penman-Monteith ETo calculation:
documentation.var_t2m_desc: Air temperature at 2m height, daily mean
documentation.var_ws_desc: Wind speed at 2m height (converted from 10m using FAO-56 Eq. 47, factor 0.748)
documentation.var_rad_desc: All-sky surface downward shortwave radiation, adjusted for elevation
documentation.sources_intro: EVAonline automatically integrates data from 6 sources. Source selection is based on the operation mode and geographic location.
documentation.sources_elevation_desc_end: (not a climate source, but essential for FAO-56 atmospheric pressure correction). Fallback: ASTER 30m for latitudes > 60°N or < 60°S.
documentation.feat_elevation_desc: Altitude obtained automatically via OpenTopoData (SRTM 30m) for atmospheric pressure correction in FAO-56.
documentation.feat_timezone_desc: Automatically detected from coordinates. All dates are adjusted to local time.
documentation.feat_fusion_desc: Adaptive Kalman Ensemble Filter fuses multiple sources with variance-based weighting. Two modes: Adaptive (Brazil, with Xavier climatology) and Simple (Global).
documentation.feat_progress_desc: Track calculation progress via WebSocket with progress bar and step-by-step messages.
documentation.feat_ocean_desc: Points over the ocean are automatically detected and calculation is blocked with a clear warning.
documentation.feat_wind_desc: Wind converted from 10m to 2m height using FAO-56 Equation 47 (factor: 0.748).
documentation.feat_preprocess_desc: Automatic outlier detection (IQR method), gap filling, and regional validation limits (Brazil: Xavier et al.; Global: conservative world limits).
documentation.feat_cache_desc: Results cached in Redis for fast repeated queries. API rate limiting to prevent abuse.
documentation.feat_usa_desc: Points in Continental USA automatically activate NWS Forecast + NWS Stations as additional data sources.
documentation.limits_intro: EVAonline is a free, open-source academic tool. To ensure fair access and protect external API quotas, the following usage limits apply:
documentation.limits_per_user_desc: Limits are tracked by IP address and reset daily at midnight (UTC).
documentation.limits_dashboard_detail: Results displayed directly in the browser
documentation.limits_historical_detail: Results sent by email with CSV/Excel attachment
documentation.limits_api_desc: Each calculation may query multiple external APIs. EVAonline monitors global daily consumption to avoid exceeding provider limits:
documentation.limits_exceeded_title: What happens when a limit is reached?
documentation.limits_exceeded_desc: A clear warning message is displayed. No data is lost. You can try again the next day or use a different mode.
documentation.limits_tip_desc: Results are cached. Repeating the exact same location and period will use cached data without counting against your limits.
documentation.faq_q1: Why do I need to provide an email for Historical mode?
documentation.faq_a1: Historical data requests can take 1-5 minutes to process (fetching from multiple APIs, preprocessing, fusion, ETo calculation). The task runs asynchronously via Celery, and results are delivered by email with CSV/Excel attachment.
documentation.faq_q2: Why is there a 2-day delay for recent data?
documentation.faq_a2: Open-Meteo Archive (ERA5-Land reanalysis) has a ~2 day latency. EVAonline automatically uses Open-Meteo Forecast to fill this gap, so you always get complete data up to today.
documentation.faq_q3: What happens if I click on the ocean?
documentation.faq_a3: The system detects ocean points automatically and blocks the calculation with a clear warning. Only land locations are supported.
documentation.faq_q4: Why are there more sources available for USA locations?
documentation.faq_a4: The Continental USA is covered by NOAA's NWS Forecast (gridded) and ~1,800 active NWS Stations with real-time hourly observations. These provide additional data for the Kalman fusion ensemble.
documentation.faq_q5: What is the Kalman Ensemble Filter?
documentation.faq_a5: An Adaptive Kalman Filter that fuses data from multiple sources, weighting each by its estimated variance. For Brazil, it uses Xavier BR-DWGD climatology (27 cities) as bias correction reference. For other locations, a Simple mode operates without climatology.
documentation.faq_a6: Validated against 17 Brazilian weather stations (Xavier et al. 2016, 2022) over 30 years. The Kalman fusion consistently outperforms any single data source. Details available in the About page.
documentation.faq_q7: What is the maximum period I can request?
documentation.faq_a7: Historical: up to 90 days per request (1990→present, 2-day delay). Recent: 7, 14, 21, or 30 days (instant). Forecast: 6 days (today → today+5).
documentation.license_name: GNU Affero General Public License v3.0

nws_card.distance_tooltip: Distance between the selected point and the weather station
nws_card.obs_unavailable: Observation not available at this time
nws_card.nws_info: Real data from NWS/NOAA station. Official page:
nws_card.reading_tooltip: Time of the most recent sensor reading from this station. The data below corresponds to that moment.
